examples: His thoughts were so elastic, his heart so tender; and involuntarily he picked one of the nearest flowers.
In March 1998, a friend of Williams's got him involuntarily confined to a treatment center for addictions, and wrote to inform the casino of Williams's gambling problem.
At current trends, it will be years before all the involuntarily unemployed return to work.
Although no one was made redundant involuntarily, in 2006 the fire-brigade union called a strike.
